files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives section, expand Prerequisites (required Linux skills), add estimated completion time, create comprehensive Key Takeaways summary with bullet points
files.01_Domain_Decomposition.md: Add Learning Objectives, expand mathematical formulation (how decomposition algorithms work), add decomposition quality metrics, include troubleshooting section (common decomposition errors), add practical exercises
files.02_Performance_Monitoring.md: Add Learning Objectives, expand monitoring tools beyond basic grep (pyFoam, external tools), add section on interpreting speedup/efficiency metrics, include performance visualization techniques, add common bottlenecks identification
files.03_Optimization_Techniques.md: CRITICAL: This has significant redundancy with 01_Domain_Decomposition.md (decomposition methods) and 02_Performance_Monitoring.md (load balancing). Refactor to focus ONLY on: solver settings optimization, I/O optimization (compression, buffering), memory optimization, and numerical scheme tuning. Remove all decomposition content - it's covered in file 01. Add Learning Objectives, add optimization workflow/decision tree, include benchmarking methodology
files.04_HPC_Integration.md: Add Learning Objectives, expand cloud platforms section (AWS, Azure, GCP specific OpenFOAM setups), add data transfer optimization, include interactive vs batch job comparison, add cost optimization tips for cloud, include security best practices, add troubleshooting HPC-specific issues

global_strategy: Apply consistent 3W Framework (What/Why/How) to all files: WHAT = clear concept definition, WHY = practical importance/benefits, HOW = step-by-step implementation. Add standard sections to each file: Learning Objectives → Prerequisites → Core Content (3W) → Practical Examples → Troubleshooting → Key Takeaways → Related Reading. Resolve content overlap: move decomposition theory exclusively to 01, monitoring exclusively to 02, keep 03 focused on solver/I/O/memory optimization only. Ensure bilingual consistency (TH/EN) throughout. Add estimated completion times and difficulty levels to enable self-paced learning.
